title: Appointment of Auditor in Company – ADT-1 Filing Online
metaDescription: Get appointment of auditor in company with ADT-1 filing online support. Know what is appointment of auditor, how to appoint auditor in company, fees & documents.

hero.headline: Appointment of Auditor in Company – Auditor Appointment & ADT-1 Filing
hero.subtext: Professionals Law ensures your auditor appointment is MCA-compliant, penalty-free and on time.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The Appointment of Auditor is a mandatory compliance requirement under Section 139(1) of the Companies Act, 2013, read with Rule 4(2) of the Companies (Audit and Auditors) Rules, 2014. Every company shall appoint their first auditor within 30 days of the date of incorporation and the auditors other than first auditor shall be appointed at every Annual General Meeting (AGM) who shall hold office from the conclusion of that AGM till the conclusion of the sixth AGM and thereafter till the conclusion of every sixth meeting. The Form ADT-1 has to be filed with the ROC within 15 days of the appointment. Professionals Law helps its clients in preparing resolutions, consent letters and submission of ADT-1 on time to be compliant with all applicable laws on audit and save themselves from penalties.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Filing Form ADT-1 is necessary for legal reporting of the auditor's appointment by the company to the government. It not only fulfills a statutory requirement but also documents transparency in the financial reporting process. Failing to file ADT-1 may attract late filing penalties and reflect poor corporate governance. Professionals Law ensures that all formalities from board resolution to MCA filing are completed accurately and promptly. This enables companies to comply with audit requirements under the law and maintain credibility with regulators, stakeholders and investors.

body.title: Legal Compliance
body.description: Timely filing of ADT-1 ensures that your company adheres to MCA norms and avoids penalties or legal scrutiny for non-compliance.

body.title: Financial Transparency
body.description: Appointment of a statutory auditor enhances the company’s credibility and builds trust among shareholders, banks and regulatory authorities.

body.title: Smooth ROC Filings
body.description: With Professionals Law, you get complete support in ROC-related filings, ensuring accurate documentation and timely submissions.

body.title: Support for Future Fundraising
body.description: Properly appointed and recorded auditors help when raising funds or applying for loans, as audited reports are a must.

body.title: Hassle-Free Renewal or Change
body.description: ADT-1 facilitates future change or reappointment of auditors by establishing proper records from the start.

criteria: All companies incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013.
The auditor must be a Chartered Accountant (CA) or a firm of CAs registered with ICAI.
The auditor should not be disqualified under Section 141 of the Companies Act, 2013.
Appointment must be approved by shareholders at the first AGM or within 30 days by the Board for the first auditor.

documents: Appointment letter to the auditor by the company
PAN and ICAI registration number of the Auditor
Details of the Company (CIN, Address, Email, Contact)

body.description: Auditor provides consent to be appointed and confirms they are not disqualified.
The Board of Directors passes a resolution for appointment in the board meeting.
In case of reappointment or regular appointment, approval is taken in the AGM or EGM.
Professionals Law prepares the ADT-1 form with all necessary attachments and declarations.
Form ADT-1 is submitted to the MCA within 15 days from the AGM or board resolution.
Obtain the MCA acknowledgement as proof of successful filing.

body.title: Late Filing Fees
body.description: Twice the normal fees for delays up to 30 days, four times for 31–60 days, six times for 61–90 days, and ten times for 91–180 days.

body.title: Penalty on Officers
body.description: Officers in default may face penalties from ₹10,000 to ₹1,00,000.

body.title: First Auditor Appointment
body.description: Within 30 days of incorporation; ADT-1 to be filed within 15 days.

body.title: Casual Vacancy Appointment
body.description: To be done within 90 days; ADT-1 must be filed within 15 days.

body.question: Is ADT-1 mandatory for the first auditor?
body.answer: The first auditor appointed by the Board within 30 days of incorporation, ADT-1 filing is not mandatory but it is highly recommended for the best secretarial practices.

body.question: When should ADT-1 be filed?
body.answer: ADT-1 must be filed within 15 days from the date of AGM or EGM in which the auditor is appointed.

body.question: Is it applicable to LLPs or OPCs?
body.answer: The filing of form ADT-1 is not applicable to LLP but it is applicable to One Person Companies.

body.question: Who can be appointed as a statutory auditor?
body.answer: Only a Chartered Accountant or a CA firm registered with ICAI is eligible.

body.question: Can the same auditor be reappointed after 5 years?
body.answer: Yes, subject to rotation rules and shareholder approval.

body.question: What happens if ADT-1 is not filed on time?
body.answer: The company is liable to pay a late fee and may face additional penalties.

body.question: Can Professionals Law assist in auditor reappointment filings too?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law assists with all types of auditor appointments, changes and reappointments.

body.question: Is the digital signature required for filing ADT-1?
body.answer: Yes, the form must be digitally signed by the director and certified by a practicing CA, CS or CMA.

body.question: How much does Professionals Law charge for ADT-1 filing?
body.answer: Professionals Law offers affordable and customized compliance packages. Contact us for a tailored quote.
